content: Language education's teaching of reflexion as a metacognitive vocabulary and critical thinking practice can be framed as a blueprint to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where 'vocabulary acquisition' of reflective prompts maps to building structured self-critique frameworks for large language models. Training LLMs to systematically internalize reflexion as a learnable skill the same way human students do—from recognizing the concept to practicing it in context—can reduce unstructured self-reflection drift and improve reasoning consistency.
